title: The Bondman.
content:
Bind me but to thee with thine hair,
    And quickly I shall be
    Made by that fetter or that snare
    A bondman unto thee.
    Or if thou tak'st that bond away,
    Then bore me through the ear,
    And by the law I ought to stay
    For ever with thee here.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "The Bondman.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
